          Companies

          CommBank

          Employment Consultant

          Busy At Work–Townsville QLD
          3.1 from 31 reviews at SEEK
          $76,000 - $76,000 a year
          Full time, Contract
          17d ago, from BUSY at Work Inc
          Apply on company site
          Save job
          Open in new tab

          23/06/2026

          22/07/2026

          Full Time - Fixed Term

          Townsville

          Community Services

          Our purpose is to make skills, education and employment accessible to every person, so they can create their own world of unbounded possibilities.

          About The BUSY Group…

          The BUSY Group is a not-for-profit organisation which presents 47 years experience as a community partner.  With multiple business entities operating across 100+ locations Australia-wide and now in New Zealand and the United Kingdom, we offer an extensive range of employment services and programs, vocational training, apprenticeships, mentoring, Inclusive Employment Australia, specialist schools and allied health services. 

          Job Description

          About the Role…

          Working with our Transition to Work contract, you will provide intensive, pre-employment support to improve the work-readiness of young people and assist them into sustainable work or education. You will conduct assessments to identify barriers of the youth and provide direction and support to ensure their journey into appropriate and sustainable employment. MOTIVATE and EMPOWER our clients through their employment journey.

          Engaging and referring participants to stakeholders and community organisations. You will place participants into employment by firstly supporting them with job search activities including interview coaching and resume preparation along with matching participants to current vacancies and reverse marketing to identified employers. By doing this you will also be engaging with employers and promoting our program participants to help unlock employment opportunities for them.

          This role full time fixed term until 11th December 2026. Working hours; Monday to Friday 8:30am-5pm based in our Charters Towers Office

          CHAMPION OUR CLIENTS; BE THE PERSON WHO BELIEVES IN THEM TODAY! 

          BUSY Benefits… 

          • Competitive salary; Circa $76k + Super + bonus
          • ACHIEVABLE and generous bonus incentive plans
          • Clear pathways for internal advancement 
          • BUSY Benefits program - Paid parental Leave, retail discounts and access to Fitness Passport
          • Enjoy work that genuinely impacts lives 
          • Employee Assistance Program with Sonder Wellbeing App
          • Tools of the trade to support your individual role 
          • Work as part of a team whilst working autonomously with your clients
          Desired Skills and Experience

          A bit about you...

          • Excellent customer and client interpersonal skills
          • Strong administrative skills
          • You are comfortable working with performance targets, KPIs, and compliance requirements.
          • You are highly organised, detail‑focused, and able to manage competing priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
          • You bring a strong client‑first mindset, with the ability to build rapport and trust with people from diverse backgrounds.
          • You build strong relationships with participants, employers, and stakeholders from diverse backgrounds.
          • Ability to obtain a Working With Children Check and National Police Check with no disclosable outcomes

          Next steps...

          This role will actively commence recruitment from the date of advertisement. The closing date of this ad is dependent on the appointment of the role.

          The BUSY Group is an organisation that prioritises the safeguarding of children and all vulnerable people by actively adopting strategies that embed a culture of zero tolerance for abuse of any kind. The appointment of successful applicants will be subject to satisfactory employment screening including criminal history and relevant working with children checks. 

          The BUSY Group is committed to achieving a diverse workforce and strongly encourages applications from First Nations People, veterans, people from culturally diverse backgrounds and people with disabilities.
